data.title: Lafayette Digital Acquisition Corp. I closes $287.5 million IPO of 28.75 million units at $10 each on Nasdaq (Ticker: ZKPU).
data.description: Lafayette Digital Acquisition Corp. I has closed its initial public offering of 28,750,000 units, including 3,750,000 units from the underwriters' over-allotment option. Priced at $10.00 per unit, the offering raised $287,500,000 for the Company. Each unit includes one Class A ordinary share and one-fourth of a redeemable warrant. The warrants will become exercisable 30 days after the initial business combination, allowing holders to purchase one Class A ordinary share at $11.50. The units are listed on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol "ZKPU," with the Class A ordinary shares and warrants expected to trade separately under "ZKP" and "ZKPW" respectively.

data.title: Accelint wins $151B SHIELD IDIQ contract with Missile Defense Agency.
data.description: Accelint recently disclosed in Orlando, Florida that it has secured a significant government contract for the Missile Defense Agency SHIELD program. The contract falls under the indefinite-delivery/indefinite-quantity (IDIQ) framework, boasting a substantial ceiling value of $151 billion. This noteworthy development underscores Accelint's successful entry into the defense sector with a promise of innovation and long-term partnership.

data.title: Space stocks, led by Firefly, soar in 2026. ASTS consolidates near all-time highs around $91-$100 after 260% rally in 2025. RKLB gains over 20% in 2026, reaching prices around $85.
data.description: Space stocks have seen a significant surge in the new year, with many companies posting double-digit gains year-to-date. Factors contributing to this rise include support from the Trump administration, technical achievements, and the potential SpaceX IPO. AST SpaceMobile, Inc. (NASDAQ: ASTS) has consolidated near all-time highs around $91-$100 after a 260% rally throughout 2025, gaining 28% year-to-date. However, some institutional analysts have expressed bearish sentiment, with Scotiabank issuing a downgrade. Rocket Lab Corp. (NASDAQ: RKLB) has also seen strong bullish momentum, with the stock surging over 65% in a month to reach prices around $85 in early January. Rocket Lab stock has gained more than 20% in 2026 alone. This information is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Sport Clips Haircuts raised $1.22M for veterans' scholarships & provided free haircuts. Total donations for VFW Foundation exceeded $16M.
data.description: The VFW Foundation has received over $16 million in donations to assist veterans with transitioning to civilian careers through education. In a press release from January 12, 2026, it was reported that Sport Clips Haircuts' Help A Hero Scholarship Campaign in 2025 contributed $1.22 million towards supporting military service members in pursuing educational opportunities.

data.description: On Monday, Eric Adams, the ex-mayor of New York City, publicly supported a digital currency named "NYC Token" at Times Square.

data.title: Bill Abbott names Paige Havins as Deputy General Counsel at Great American Media, adding her 10+ years of legal and transaction experience. She previously held the same role at a global tech protection company. Havins is also an adjunct law professor with a focus on mentorship and community service.
data.description: With over ten years of expertise in legal affairs and transactions, the individual has served as the former Deputy General Counsel at a leading global technology security firm. They also hold a role as an adjunct law professor, demonstrating a dedicated focus on mentorship and community involvement. The professional is based in New York and the information was released on January 12, 2026, by PRNewswire through Great American Media.

data.title: World Liberty Financial, linked to Trump, launches $3.4 billion World Liberty Markets lending platform with $1 stablecoin. Critics raise conflict of interest concerns.
data.description: World Liberty Financial (CRYPTO: WLFI), supported by President Donald Trump's family, has introduced World Liberty Markets, a decentralized finance (DeFi) lending platform for its stablecoin valued at $1, amounting to $3.4 billion. However, critics have raised concerns over potential conflicts of interest relating to Trump's substantial $800 million cryptocurrency earnings in 2025. The platform enables users to engage in lending and borrowing operations using the USD1 stablecoin, which can be utilized as collateral alongside assets such as Ethereum (CRYPTO: ETH), USDC, USDT, tokenized Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC), and the WLFI governance token. Operated by the Dolomite protocol, users can currently either lend out or borrow USD1 at approximately 0.08% and 0.83% interest rates, respectively. It is anticipated that these rates will fluctuate as more capital flows into the system. Since its inception in March 2025, the USD1 stablecoin has witnessed a circulation surge to $3.4 billion, positioning it among the prominent dollar-backed stablecoins, trailing behind leading players like Tether and Circle (NYSE).

data.title: Abercrombie & Fitch's stock dropped in heavy volume as company lowers full-year outlook to lower end of guidance.
data.description: A popular fashion retailer, Abercrombie & Fitch, experienced significant selling activity as its stock was traded heavily following a downward revision in its full-year forecast. The company adjusted its outlook to the lower range of its initial guidance, leading to a decline in its share price. This shift in performance comes amidst a broader trend of softening results among fashion retailers, marking a notable departure from their previous strong performance.
